titleStmt.title: A week of wetness in La Vergne

sourceDesc.p: Downloaded from https://medium.com/@louBurnard/a-week-of-wetness-in-la-vergne-a412b6564d8b and translated from HTML to TEI

head.hi: A week of wetness in La Vergne

body.p: Sun shines! We stay in bed to make sure it’s not an april fool, then get up as usual. I try on my blue boiler suit, bought last year at a jumble sale, but it fits. Spend rest of day pottering around garden, mostly. Plant out herbs bought yesterday in pots; destroy some snowdrops to make space for raspberry bush ditto, in front. Formulate cunning plan to repurpose old meat cupboard in the shed as safe house for lettuces, and proceed to implement same. This involves some minor d.i.y. but is satisfyingly done by lunch time. Which is roast lamb because that’s what Lilette took out of the freezer yesterday thinking it was chicken, but none the worse for that. She has also made bread pudding. After lunch, paint the Lettuce House, chat to neighbours who are busy erecting a new terrasse, plant out some onions. Reflect over cup of tea that this is more than enough for one day, so maybe I should start diarizing (which I am).

Up earlyish for TAD which is driven by strange old man who seems uncertain of route, and asks me to navigate to his first pick up point, at Le Monteil which I duly do with the aid of M Google, but alas Mme Chloe doesn’t show up, so we set off to Gueret, and the market, which is still much the same, tho wet. Veg, bread, cheese, plants for the garden, anything else? Ah yes, milk. Coffee in :e Moderne to shelter from the rain, and then back we go, in time for lunch of choucroute and potatos dauphinoises from the traiteurs. Light stove. Fall asleep. Outside it continues to rain, but waits to hail till L insists on going out for a walk. That’ll teach her.
Did lots more work on ELTeC tutorial prep. L made pasta for lunch. We snoozed. We ordered a brand new turkish style tapis. Watched rather good Chabrol film La femme infidele on telly.

L went by TAD alone to Gueret; I walked to Jean-Luc’s to get gas, wine, and water, thus making myself late for lunch prep. I prepared potato pie, which always takes longer to heat up than you think, and sausages, which always take less. Worked on ELTEC tutorial.

A day of washing machine business. In the morning cheerful young man arrives to take away old machine and install new one. Much clucking and tutting to see that I am disposing of a genuine Miele museum piece like what they don’t make them any more. New machine is finally wheeled into place and appears to work. In the afternoon M Leffort came and took away the dryer for examination. He also managed to impress on me what a shame it was I had bought a Hotpoint washing machine. Dammit all, at least it works.

By TAD to Gueret Gare. The idea was to take the E bus to E Leclerc, but when it came it looked like an F bus so we missed it. So we walked, which really wasn’t far, expecially if you stop for coffee on the way to avoid getting entirely soaked. At Mr Leclerc’s emporium we purchased a top loading Hotpoint washing machine on special offer (199 euro) which the man agreed to deliver it for free, the next day, by the Lord. Eleclerc is a cavern of consumerist joys, though virtually nobody is actually working or shopping. We checked out the bookshop until it was time for lunch, which meant finding our way out of there and down the hill to the Campanile restaurant, attaced to the motel by the motorway. This bit of Gueret is not intended for pedestrians. I had the 3 course buffet, L had the two course; both of us got stuffed, and then staggered back up the hill, L to go grocery shopping., and I to go shoe shopping. Neither totally successful nor entirely unsuccessful in either case. M Leffort phones and we make a date for tomorrow, so he can give his view on the defunct dryer. The Tad arrives eventually and takes us home for a well deserved snooze.

Walk squelchily into Bussiere, for lunch at Benoit’s. Fish and chips for me, daube flamande for L, which turns out to be rather nice, and consoling on a wet day. Johan appears just as we are setting off for the walk back, and gives us a ride home. He then explains that they don’t do electro menager, and I need to talk to Alain Leffort in St Vaury about my dead washing machine, which I duly do. He doesn’t answer of course, because it’s Monday. No matter. We have a plan to go to Gueret and buy a new one. Continue to roast selves in front of fire.

Beautiful spring sunshine to console us for the dampness within doors. I lug the sodden carpet into the garden and light the stove again. Richard turns up, inspects the damage, explains the error of my ways to me, fixes up a temporary solution so that we can turn the water on again, disappears to get a spare part from some other job while we have lunch. Later on we realise that the washing machine is also kaput : makes sad clunking noises when it should be spinning. And won’t open. Phone Johan again to ask for help.

Up early to get the 0845 to get the 1130 eurostar … plenty of time to cross Paris and pick up a few groceries at Monop before catching the 1640 train to La Souterraine. Which is on time, and crowded only at our end, as I discover when going in search of the buffet ambulant. And when we arrive, here is Lisa come to drive us home in time. The familiar pattern all going well, till we actually arrive, and I open the front door to hear the sound of running water. A pipe in the bathroom has burst, and we have a small flood to contend with. Turning off the mains down the hole is evidently still necessary: Lisa helps me do it, waits patiently while we contend with the forces of dark panic, leave a message for Jonathan (he’s away this weekend), she will contact Richard to see if he can help, then drives us to Benoit’s to get some supper and consolation. And so to the Tilleule, where we are in time for consolatory dinner (burgers and beer) and Corinne kindly gives us a lift home. Into our chilly bed, thinking of adventures ahead.
